beats.narration: Finding a disbond is the easy part. It isn't finished until it's measured, compared to written criteria, and dispositioned on paper.

payload.title: DISPOSITION IT
payload.sub: Found is not finished until it's dispositioned on paper.

beats.narration: Nearly every bond specification builds from four bricks: a recording threshold, a maximum single size, a cumulative density limit, and location rules.

payload.heading: The four bricks of bond acceptance

beats.narration: On the C-scan, apply all four: log recordables, check the single-size limit, total area against the density limit, and police the edge zones.

payload.heading: Apply the criteria on the C-scan

beats.narration: Your verdict is accept, record, or reject against the written criteria. Anything outside the limits goes to engineering. You never waive, and repairs re-inspect.

chips.t: Repairs re-inspect to the same standard

beats.narration: Last habit: read the acceptance criteria before the probe touches the part, and scan to answer them. Done right, evaluation designs the inspection.
